name: CKM mixing: Wolfenstein lambda-hierarchy compression
requirement: Any candidate flavor mechanism must reproduce the CKM matrix hierarchical structure |V_us| ~ lambda, |V_cb| ~ A*lambda^2, |V_ub| ~ A*lambda^3*sqrt(rho^2 + eta^2), |V_td| ~ A*lambda^3*sqrt((1-rho)^2 + eta^2), with a single small expansion parameter lambda ~ 0.225 and order-unity coefficients.
what_it_excludes: A flavor mechanism that predicts O(1) mixing angles (anarchic CKM) is excluded. The hierarchical pattern is too pronounced to be a fluctuation.

regime.description: M_Z scale; CKM elements are quoted at this scale by convention.

parameterization.name: Wolfenstein parameterization
parameterization.summary: V_CKM expanded in powers of lambda = sin(theta_C) ~ 0.225, with coefficients A ~ 0.81 and (rho, eta) describing the CP-violating apex of the unitarity triangle.

procedural.predicted_observable: |V_us|, |V_cb|, |V_ub|, |V_td| should fit lambda^n hierarchy to leading order in lambda.
framework_inputs: Predicted hierarchy in CKM elements at the matching scale
Identification of expansion parameter (must be a single small parameter, not multiple unrelated small parameters)
Prediction or post-diction of the CP-violating phase magnitude
procedural.compression_ansatz: V_CKM = 1 + lambda * V_1 + lambda^2 * V_2 + lambda^3 * V_3 + ... with O(1) coefficients in V_n. Equivalent to integer Froggatt-Nielsen charges of the left-handed quark doublets {q_Q_1, q_Q_2, q_Q_3} = {3, 2, 0} (or similar) with epsilon = lambda.
free_parameters: A, rho_bar, eta_bar (three additional Wolfenstein parameters)
procedural.notes: Compression ratio: nine real parameters in V_CKM (4 angles + 1 phase, with unitarity reducing the rank) compressed to four parameters (lambda, A, rho_bar, eta_bar) once the lambda^n hierarchy is recognised. A flavor mechanism passes by deriving the structure from a higher-level construct; it fails by having to put the hierarchy in by hand.

primary_references.citation: Wolfenstein, 'Parametrization of the Kobayashi-Maskawa Matrix', PRL 51, 1945 (1983)
Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics' - CKM section (2024)
